scope.setting: finite cutoff spherical modes with diagonal screen algebra C^N, observer bridge algebra M_N, and accepted positive-definite static-patch Schur channels
scope.result_type: finite regulator-class universality and perturbative stability for the screen-shadow no-go and M_N versus C^N bridge distinction
scope.bounded_family: 1 <= L <= 5, N=(L+1)^2, four accepted regulators, perturbation radius 0.05
scope.claim_boundary: finite regulator universality only; not continuum de Sitter quantum gravity, dS/CFT, or literal ER=EPR in de Sitter

core_claim: Across a declared class of finite static-patch Schur regulators, screen entropy, low-order diagonal correlator, horizon-overlap, and screen-restricted transfer shadows collide between quantum and classical controls, while off-diagonal response separates M_N from C^N.

accepted_regulators.source: Gaussian modular-time average / finite Lindblad pure dephasing
CP/TP-completed Euclidean heat/cap transfer on energy differences

exact_finite_theorem_style_claims.claim: Accepted regulators are finite energy-difference Schur channels with unit diagonal, analytic complete-positivity proof, trace preservation, unitality, double-scaled damping, and nonzero off-diagonal retention.
The accepted quantum regulators and the classical dephased control agree on screen entropy, low-order diagonal correlators, horizon-overlap data, and screen-restricted transfer data.
The accepted quantum regulators certify an epsilon-recoverable M_N bridge by nonzero off-diagonal response, while the dephased control has bridge algebra C^N.
Spectrum, coupling, KMS/temperature, and cutoff-geometry perturbations of radius 0.05 preserve CPTP/unitality, screen-shadow collision, and off-diagonal bridge response for the bounded family.
Raw nonunital Euclidean heat transfer and screen-only dS/CFT-like maps are recorded as obstructions, not promoted to observer bridge channels.

bounded_certificate_evidence.claim: The certificate checks cutoffs 1 through 5 for all accepted regulators and declared perturbation variants.
Focused tests cover accepted class membership, CPTP audits, bridge-algebra separation, perturbation stability, obstruction rows, scaling bounds, and input validation.

numeric_audit_note: Complete positivity is certified analytically from positive-definite time averages or finite random-unitary/Stinespring constructions. Bounded Cholesky PSD checks are numeric sanity checks only and can be near-singular at larger cutoffs.
expert_feedback_summary: Goal 27 turns the Goal 26 single derived finite dynamics into a finite-regulator universality statement: fuzzy heat, finite phase-kick, KMS/Cauchy, and CP-completed Euclidean Schur regulators all preserve the same screen-shadow no-go and M_N versus C^N bridge-algebra distinction under bounded perturbations.
limitations: The result is a finite regulator-class theorem. It does not show that continuum de Sitter static-patch physics, dS/CFT, or quantum gravity selects this class.
